techniques.evidence_quote: The incident originated from a likely exploitation of a known remote code execution vulnerability in a Jenkins instance, an open source software development automation server.
the threat actor created multiple IAM user accounts

deploying Linux and Windows virtual machines in new EC2 instances in the compromised environment... The attacker deployed the additional EC2 instances in a different AWS region than that used by the victim
Logs availability has been an issue due to the lack of CloudTrail log retention
The server was hosted in an Amazon Web Service (AWS) environment and had a hardcoded root access key.
After thorough environment and users' enumeration, the attacker was able to obtain the password for another G-Suite user account
deploying Linux and Windows virtual machines in new EC2 instances in the compromised environment to scan and exploit external IP addresses

techniques.technique_name: Search Open Technical Databases: Scan Databases
techniques.evidence_quote: the attacker likely used Shodan to identify other vulnerable Jenkins instances online
deploying Linux and Windows virtual machines in new EC2 instances in the compromised environment to scan and exploit external IP addresses
accessed internal data, including those stored in S3 buckets via the free Windows client S3 Browser
accessed internal data, including those stored in S3 buckets via the free Windows client S3 Browser
threat actors attempted to cover their tracks by deleting the company's entire production environment, all hosted on AWS, and the backup copies
